cards.title: Managing In-Laws' Behavior Before Having Children
cards.body: A reader worries about her partner's cruel mother and how she'll treat future grandchildren. An advice columnist recommends minimizing contact unless the grandmother makes radical changes.

cards.title: Family Estrangement Effects on Wellbeing
cards.body: Research shows estrangement exists on a continuum rather than as a binary choice. Support plays an important role in family estrangement, whether people seek reconciliation or not.

cards.title: Becoming an Uncle Healed Childhood Bullying
cards.body: A man who experienced homophobia as a boy found healing through his relationship with his nieces and nephews. His nibling relationships transformed his sense of family and self.

cards.title: Childcare Affordability and Government Support
cards.body: The government is spending billions to help families access childcare. Officials warn that hidden charges may prevent families from seeing the full benefit.

cards.title: Using Empathy to Connect With Children
cards.body: Language that witnesses rather than dismisses creates deeper human connection. Engaging and connecting language works better than correcting in parent-child relationships.

cards.title: Emotional Regulation Skills for Parenting
cards.body: Coping skills learned in childhood often don't work in adulthood. Parents can upgrade their emotional regulation to better manage stress and model healthy behavior.

cards.title: Regret in Fatherhood: A Teacher for Growth
cards.body: Regret, though painful, awakens fathers to their shortcomings and guides them toward repair. This emotion helps men become wiser and more present parents.

cards.title: How Childhood Models Shape Adult Parenting
cards.body: The models and coping skills imprinted on us as children often need updating. Understanding these patterns helps parents control emotions and use them as information.

cards.title: Cosmeticorexia: Girls and Skincare Obsession
cards.body: Social media is fueling a boom in children's skincare products and obsessive beauty routines. Experts worry about long-term psychological impacts on girls' self-image.

cards.title: Neuroscience of Kindness in Child Development
cards.body: Screaming at traffic or technology wires brains for anger and stress. Everyday kindness protects mental peace and models healthy emotional responses for children.
